message:
mcp-server v0.3 partial: verify+explain intents + stats resources +
GitHub Actions for CI / Python wheels / Node binaries (one tighter loop)

Three deliverables this iteration (user said "tighter loops"):

## v0.3a — verify + explain intents

- `intent: "verify"` — read on-disk bundle (RuLakeBundle::read_from_dir),
  recompute SHAKE-256 witness, compare against cache pointer
  (cache_witness_of). Witness-fail-closed: invalid bundle returns
  Ok(refusal) with reason_code=WitnessMismatchRefused, NEVER serves.
  trust_level: Verified | Partial | Unverified depending on cache vs
  disk state.
- `intent: "explain"` — cache_stats() + cache_stats_by_backend +
  cache_stats_by_collection rollup. Returns the rollup string in
  decision.reason for the agent to read; data carries the headline
  hit_rate as the score for the trivial parse case.
- `intent: "refresh"` still returns RULAKE_INTERNAL — ships in v0.3b
  with the publish capability tier.
- 4 new smoke tests; 8/8 smoke + 7/7 HTTP = 15/15 pass.

## v0.3b — `rulake://stats` + `rulake://stats/by-backend` resources

- ServerHandler::list_resources + read_resource implemented.
- ServerCapabilities now advertises both tools and resources.
- v0.2 returns fresh stats per read; the 200ms TTL caching from
  ADR-004 §Resources is a v0.3 optimization (the lock+clone cost is
  sub-microsecond — TTL only matters at extreme poll rates).
- End-to-end JSON-RPC verified: resources/list returns both with
  mimeType: application/json; resources/read returns the stats body.

## v0.3c — GitHub Actions

Three workflows:
- ci.yml — every push/PR. Builds + tests all 5 sibling crates
  (root, mcp-server, gcs-backend, python, node). Submodules: recursive.
- release-python.yml — tag `python-v*` triggers ABI3 wheel build
  matrix (manylinux_2_28 x86_64/aarch64, macOS x86_64/arm64, Windows
  x86_64) via PyO3/maturin-action@v1, plus sdist (which includes
  vendored sources via [tool.maturin].include). PyPI publish via
  trusted OIDC publishing (preferred over PYPI_API_TOKEN).
- release-node.yml — tag `node-v*` triggers @napi-rs/cli per-platform
  build (5 triples). Publishes per-platform sub-packages
  @ruvector/rulake-<triple> + the umbrella @ruvector/rulake with
  matching optionalDependencies. Prisma / next-swc pattern.

YAML parsed clean (python3 yaml.safe_load).

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+# Continuous integration — every push and PR.
+#
+# Builds and tests every shipped crate so a green main is "everything
+# works on Linux x86_64". Per-platform release builds live in the
+# release-* workflows.
+
+name: ci
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main]
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
+
+jobs:
+  rust:
+    name: Rust — ${{ matrix.crate }}
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        # The five sibling Cargo packages per ADR-001.
+        crate: [".", "mcp-server", "gcs-backend", "python", "node"]
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive   # ADR-001 requires vendor/ruvector
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          workspaces: ${{ matrix.crate }}
+      - name: Build
+        working-directory: ${{ matrix.crate }}
+        run: cargo build --release
+      - name: Test
+        # The python/ + node/ crates have no Rust-side tests of their
+        # own (their tests run in pytest / node --test). Skip in those
+        # rows; the release-python / release-node workflows cover them.
+        if: matrix.crate != 'python' && matrix.crate != 'node'
+        working-directory: ${{ matrix.crate }}
+        run: cargo test --release
+
+  python-tests:
+    name: Python SDK — pytest
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+        with:
+          python-version: "3.12"
+      - name: Install maturin + test deps
+        run: pip install maturin pytest numpy
+      - name: Build + test
+        working-directory: python
+        run: |
+          maturin develop --release
+          pytest tests/ -v
+
+  node-tests:
+    name: Node SDK — node --test
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+      - name: Build native binding
+        working-directory: node
+        run: |
+          cargo build --release
+          cp target/release/libruvector_rulake_node.so rulake.linux-x64-gnu.node
+      - name: Run smoke tests
+        working-directory: node
+        run: node --test __test__/smoke.test.mjs

@@ -0,0 +1,119 @@
+# Release Node SDK — per-platform .node binaries via napi-rs (ADR-003 §5).
+#
+# Triggered manually (workflow_dispatch) or on a tag matching
+# `node-v*` (e.g. node-v2.2.0). Builds five platform binaries and
+# publishes one umbrella package + five `optionalDependencies`
+# packages on npm — the Prisma / next-swc pattern.
+#
+# Publishes when NPM_TOKEN is set in repo secrets.
+
+name: release-node
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "node-v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      publish:
+        description: "Publish to npm"
+        type: boolean
+        default: false
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  PACKAGE_DIR: node
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    name: build (${{ matrix.target.triple }})
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.target.os }}
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        target:
+          - { os: ubuntu-latest,   triple: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  artifact: rulake.linux-x64-gnu.node }
+          - { os: ubuntu-latest,   triple: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, artifact: rulake.linux-arm64-gnu.node }
+          - { os: macos-13,        triple: x86_64-apple-darwin,        artifact: rulake.darwin-x64.node }
+          - { os: macos-14,        triple: aarch64-apple-darwin,       artifact: rulake.darwin-arm64.node }
+          - { os: windows-latest,  triple: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,     artifact: rulake.win32-x64-msvc.node }
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+        with:
+          targets: ${{ matrix.target.triple }}
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+      - name: Cross-compile shim (linux-aarch64 only)
+        if: matrix.target.triple == 'aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu'
+        run: |
+          sudo apt-get update
+          sudo apt-get install -y gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu
+          echo 'CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_UNKNOWN_LINUX_GNU_LINKER=aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc' >> $GITHUB_ENV
+      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          workspaces: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+      - name: Install @napi-rs/cli
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        run: npm install --no-save @napi-rs/cli@^2.18.4
+      - name: Build .node binary
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        run: npx napi build --platform --release --target ${{ matrix.target.triple }}
+      - name: Stage artifact
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          # napi build emits the binary in the package root with the
+          # right filename based on --target; rename if needed.
+          if [ ! -f "${{ matrix.target.artifact }}" ]; then
+            ls -la *.node 2>/dev/null || true
+            echo "expected ${{ matrix.target.artifact }} not produced; falling back to first .node"
+            mv $(ls *.node | head -1) ${{ matrix.target.artifact }}
+          fi
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: node-binary-${{ matrix.target.triple }}
+          path: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}/${{ matrix.target.artifact }}
+
+  publish:
+    name: publish to npm
+    needs: build
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    if: |
+      startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/node-v') ||
+      (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.publish == 'true')
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
+      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          path: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}/artifacts
+      - name: Stage binaries into the umbrella package
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        run: |
+          shopt -s globstar
+          for f in artifacts/**/*.node; do
+            cp "$f" .
+          done
+          ls -la *.node
+      - name: Publish per-platform packages + umbrella
+        working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+        env:
+          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          # @napi-rs/cli's `napi prepublish --skip-gh-release` walks
+          # the napi.triples in package.json, builds per-platform
+          # sub-packages (@ruvector/rulake-<triple>) and publishes
+          # them, then publishes the umbrella package with the right
+          # optionalDependencies. The Prisma / next-swc pattern.
+          npm install --no-save @napi-rs/cli@^2.18.4
+          npx napi prepublish --tag latest --skip-gh-release
+          npm publish --access public

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+# Release Python SDK — ABI3 wheels per ADR-002 §5.
+#
+# Triggered manually (workflow_dispatch) or on a tag matching
+# `python-v*` (e.g. python-v2.2.0). Builds five wheels:
+# - manylinux_2_28 x86_64
+# - manylinux_2_28 aarch64
+# - macOS x86_64 (Intel)
+# - macOS aarch64 (Apple Silicon)
+# - Windows x86_64
+# - sdist (always)
+#
+# Publishes to PyPI when PYPI_API_TOKEN is set in repo secrets.
+
+name: release-python
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "python-v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      publish:
+        description: "Publish to PyPI"
+        type: boolean
+        default: false
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  PACKAGE_DIR: python
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    name: build (${{ matrix.platform.target }})
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.platform.os }}
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        platform:
+          - { os: ubuntu-latest,    target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  manylinux: 2_28, name: linux-x86_64 }
+          - { os: ubuntu-latest,    target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, manylinux: 2_28, name: linux-aarch64 }
+          - { os: macos-13,         target: x86_64-apple-darwin,                       name: macos-x86_64 }
+          - { os: macos-14,         target: aarch64-apple-darwin,                      name: macos-aarch64 }
+          - { os: windows-latest,   target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,                    name: windows-x86_64 }
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+        with:
+          python-version: "3.12"
+      - name: Build wheel via maturin-action
+        uses: PyO3/maturin-action@v1
+        with:
+          working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+          target: ${{ matrix.platform.target }}
+          manylinux: ${{ matrix.platform.manylinux || 'auto' }}
+          args: --release --out dist
+          # ADR-002 requires ABI3-py39: one wheel per platform per release.
+          # `extension-module` is set in pyproject.toml.
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: wheel-${{ matrix.platform.name }}
+          path: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}/dist/*.whl
+
+  sdist:
+    name: build sdist
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive    # critical — pyproject.toml [tool.maturin].include pulls vendored sources
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+        with:
+          python-version: "3.12"
+      - name: Build sdist
+        uses: PyO3/maturin-action@v1
+        with:
+          working-directory: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}
+          command: sdist
+          args: --out dist
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: sdist
+          path: ${{ env.PACKAGE_DIR }}/dist/*.tar.gz
+
+  publish:
+    name: publish to PyPI
+    needs: [build, sdist]
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    # Only publish on tag push or explicit workflow_dispatch with publish=true.
+    if: |
+      startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/python-v') ||
+      (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.publish == 'true')
+    environment:
+      name: pypi
+      url: https://pypi.org/p/ruvector-rulake
+    permissions:
+      id-token: write       # OIDC trusted publishing (preferred over PYPI_API_TOKEN)
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          path: dist
+          merge-multiple: true
+      - uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
+        with:
+          packages-dir: dist
+          # Falls back to PYPI_API_TOKEN secret if OIDC isn't configured.
+          # password: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}

@@ -31,6 +31,14 @@ pub struct QueryRequest {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub search: Option<SearchArgs>,
 
+    /// Verify-intent args. Required when `intent == "verify"`.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub verify: Option<VerifyArgs>,
+
+    /// Explain-intent args (all optional).
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub explain: Option<ExplainArgs>,
+
     /// `low | medium | high`. Shapes the budget cap and policy floor.
     #[serde(default = "default_risk")]
     pub risk: Risk,
@@ -82,6 +90,34 @@ pub struct SearchArgs {
     pub k: u32,
 }
 
+#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
+pub struct VerifyArgs {
+    /// Path to the directory containing `table.rulake.json`.
+    /// Must resolve under the operator's path-allow-list (v0.3 will
+    /// enforce; v0.2 trusts the operator).
+    pub bundle_dir: String,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema, Default)]
+pub struct ExplainArgs {
+    /// Number of recent decisions to include. v0.2 ignores this and
+    /// returns the rollup; v0.3 wires the ring buffer.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub last_n_decisions: Option<u32>,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub include_per_collection_stats: bool,
+}
+
+/// Internal — output from the verify planner branch.
+struct VerifyOutcome {
+    disk_witness: String,
+    cache_witness: Option<String>,
+    recomputed_ok: bool,
+    matches_cache: bool,
+    #[allow(dead_code)]
+    bundle_dim: usize,
+}
+
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Deserialize, JsonSchema, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
 #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
 pub enum Risk {
@@ -262,15 +298,213 @@ impl Planner {
         let start = std::time::Instant::now();
         match req.intent {
             Intent::Search => self.handle_search(req, start).await,
-            Intent::Verify | Intent::Explain | Intent::Refresh => {
-                Err(PlanError::Internal(format!(
-                    "intent {:?} is not implemented in v0.1 (ADR-004 §Open questions)",
-                    req.intent
-                )))
-            }
+            Intent::Verify => self.handle_verify(req, start).await,
+            Intent::Explain => self.handle_explain(req, start).await,
+            Intent::Refresh => Err(PlanError::Internal(format!(
+                "intent {:?} is not implemented in v0.2 (lands in v0.3 with mutation tools + publish capability)",
+                req.intent
+            ))),
         }
     }
 
+    /// `intent: "verify"` — read the on-disk bundle for a route, recompute
+    /// the witness, compare against the cache pointer. ADR-004 §4a.
+    async fn handle_verify(
+        &self,
+        req: QueryRequest,
+        start: std::time::Instant,
+    ) -> Result<QueryResponse, PlanError> {
+        let v = req
+            .verify
+            .ok_or_else(|| PlanError::Internal("intent=verify requires `verify` block".into()))?;
+        let routes = match self.resolve_routes(&req.target, req.budget.max_backends as usize) {
+            Ok(r) => r,
+            Err(PlanError::Refused(resp)) => return Ok(resp),
+            Err(e) => return Err(e),
+        };
+        if routes.is_empty() {
+            return Ok(self.build_refusal_intent(
+                "verify",
+                "no allowed routes",
+                ReasonCode::PolicyRefusedAllowlist,
+                vec![], vec![], start, req.budget.max_latency_ms,
+            ));
+        }
+
+        let dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(&v.bundle_dir);
+        let bundle_dir = dir.clone();
+        let lake = Arc::clone(&self.lake);
+        let routes_for_run = routes.clone();
+        let submit = self
+            .workers
+            .submit(move || -> Result<VerifyOutcome, ruvector_rulake::RuLakeError> {
+                let bundle = ruvector_rulake::RuLakeBundle::read_from_dir(&bundle_dir)?;
+                let recomputed_ok = bundle.verify_witness();
+                // Compare disk-witness against the cache pointer for the first route.
+                let cache_witness = routes_for_run.first().and_then(|(b, c)| {
+                    lake.cache_witness_of(&(b.clone(), c.clone()))
+                });
+                let matches_cache = cache_witness.as_deref() == Some(bundle.rvf_witness.as_str());
+                Ok(VerifyOutcome {
+                    disk_witness: bundle.rvf_witness.clone(),
+                    cache_witness,
+                    recomputed_ok,
+                    matches_cache,
+                    bundle_dim: bundle.dim,
+                })
+            })
+            .await;
+
+        let outcome = match submit {
+            Ok(Ok(o)) => o,
+            Ok(Err(e)) => {
+                // Witness recompute failure / read failure → fail-closed
+                // refusal (ADR-004 §4 second-row of the two-mode table).
+                return Ok(self.build_refusal_intent(
+                    "verify",
+                    &format!("bundle read/verify failed: {e}"),
+                    ReasonCode::WitnessMismatchRefused,
+                    routes.iter().map(|(b, _)| b.clone()).collect(),
+                    routes
+                        .iter()
+                        .map(|(b, c)| Refusal {
+                            route: [b.clone(), c.clone()],
+                            code: "BUNDLE_READ_FAIL".into(),
+                        })
+                        .collect(),
+                    start,
+                    req.budget.max_latency_ms,
+                ));
+            }
+            Err(SubmitError::Degraded { inflight, cap }) => {
+                return Err(PlanError::Degraded { inflight, cap });
+            }
+            Err(e) => return Err(PlanError::Internal(format!("worker: {e}"))),
+        };
+
+        let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() * 1000.0;
+        let trust_level = if outcome.recomputed_ok && outcome.matches_cache {
+            TrustLevel::Verified
+        } else if outcome.recomputed_ok {
+            TrustLevel::Partial // disk valid, cache divergent
+        } else {
+            TrustLevel::Unverified
+        };
+        let reason_code = if !outcome.recomputed_ok {
+            ReasonCode::WitnessMismatchRefused
+        } else if outcome.matches_cache {
+            ReasonCode::CacheHitFresh
+        } else {
+            ReasonCode::StaleCacheRemoteValid
+        };
+        let backends_planned: Vec<String> =
+            routes.iter().map(|(b, _)| b.clone()).collect();
+        Ok(QueryResponse {
+            data: vec![], // verify has no row data; only metadata
+            provenance: Provenance {
+                witness: Some(outcome.disk_witness.clone()),
+                witness_verified: outcome.recomputed_ok,
+                consistency: self.consistency_label.clone(),
+                served_from: backends_planned.clone(),
+                lineage_id: None,
+            },
+            trust_level,
+            decision: Decision {
+                intent: "verify".into(),
+                chosen_action: "verify_bundle".into(),
+                reason_code,
+                reason: format!(
+                    "disk witness {} ({}); cache witness {}",
+                    outcome.disk_witness,
+                    if outcome.recomputed_ok { "valid" } else { "INVALID" },
+                    outcome.cache_witness.as_deref().unwrap_or("absent"),
+                ),
+                backends_planned: backends_planned.clone(),
+                backends_used: backends_planned,
+                consistency_used: self.consistency_label.clone(),
+                budget_used_ms: elapsed_ms,
+                budget_cap_ms: req.budget.max_latency_ms,
+                degraded: false,
+                refusals: vec![],
+            },
+        })
+    }
+
+    /// `intent: "explain"` — return cache stats + per-collection rollup
+    /// for the routes the agent named, so a downstream agent can ask
+    /// "why did the last query degrade?". ADR-004 §4a.
+    async fn handle_explain(
+        &self,
+        req: QueryRequest,
+        start: std::time::Instant,
+    ) -> Result<QueryResponse, PlanError> {
+        let _ = req.explain.unwrap_or_default(); // accept but don't act on last_n_decisions in v0.2
+        let stats = self.lake.cache_stats();
+        let by_backend = self.lake.cache_stats_by_backend();
+        let by_collection = self.lake.cache_stats_by_collection();
+        let entry_count = self.lake.cache_entry_count();
+        let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() * 1000.0;
+
+        let lines = vec![
+            format!(
+                "cache: hits={} misses={} primes={} hit_rate={:.3}",
+                stats.hits,
+                stats.misses,
+                stats.primes,
+                stats.hit_rate().unwrap_or(0.0),
+            ),
+            format!("entries: {entry_count}"),
+            format!("backends: {}", by_backend.len()),
+            format!("collections: {}", by_collection.len()),
+        ];
+
+        let backends_planned: Vec<String> = self.backend_ids.clone();
+        Ok(QueryResponse {
+            data: vec![HitJson {
+                backend: "_explain".into(),
+                collection: "_stats".into(),
+                id: "0".into(),
+                score: stats.hit_rate().unwrap_or(0.0) as f32,
+            }],
+            provenance: Provenance {
+                witness: None,
+                witness_verified: false,
+                consistency: self.consistency_label.clone(),
+                served_from: vec![],
+                lineage_id: None,
+            },
+            trust_level: TrustLevel::Verified, // pure stats; no data trust question
+            decision: Decision {
+                intent: "explain".into(),
+                chosen_action: "cache_stats_rollup".into(),
+                reason_code: ReasonCode::CacheHitFresh,
+                reason: lines.join("; "),
+                backends_planned: backends_planned.clone(),
+                backends_used: backends_planned,
+                consistency_used: self.consistency_label.clone(),
+                budget_used_ms: elapsed_ms,
+                budget_cap_ms: req.budget.max_latency_ms,
+                degraded: false,
+                refusals: vec![],
+            },
+        })
+    }
+
+    fn build_refusal_intent(
+        &self,
+        intent: &str,
+        reason: &str,
+        code: ReasonCode,
+        backends_planned: Vec<String>,
+        refusals: Vec<Refusal>,
+        start: std::time::Instant,
+        budget_cap_ms: u64,
+    ) -> QueryResponse {
+        let mut r = self.build_refusal(reason, code, backends_planned, refusals, start, budget_cap_ms);
+        r.decision.intent = intent.to_string();
+        r
+    }
+
